channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Food delivery driver helped stop suspect following deadly Austria stabbing: officials
item.description: A 23-year-old with possible connections to the Islamic State group is in custody after a stabbing of six passersby that left a 14-year-old boy dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Turkish opposition party delegation meets with Kurdish leader in Iraq as part of PKK peace efforts
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — A Turkish opposition party delegation arrived in Iraq’s semiautonomous Kurdish region Sunday against the backdrop of peace efforts between Ankara and a banned Kurdish separatist movement in Turkey.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA and ÇINAR KIPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Some in Hurricane Helene-ravaged North Carolina embrace Trump's push to abolish FEMA
item.description: SWANNANOA, N.C. (AP) — Emily Russell remembers feeling hopeful after she managed to get an appointment with the Federal Emergency Management Agency not long after Hurricane Helene ripped though her home in Swannanoa, North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has disbanded an FBI task force focused on investigating foreign influence campaigns and placed on leave several election security employees at the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Agency.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson And Christina A. Cassidy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump plans to attend NASCAR's Daytona 500 for the second time as president
item.description: Donald Trump is planning to attend Sunday's Daytona 500 for the second time as president and help further burnish his image as a sports fan.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The 'Saturday Night Live' 50th anniversary celebration is finally here. Here's how to watch
item.description: Fifty seasons of “Saturday Night Live” sketches, songs and special guests will be celebrated Sunday as the variety show celebrates its landmark anniversary.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Suspect in Tupac Shakur killing seeks to delay trial as defense identifies new witnesses
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Attorneys for the man suspected of killing rap icon Tupac Shakur want to delay next month’s trial, saying more time is needed for investigative work to ensure that Duane “Keffe D” Davis gets a fair trial.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: Wyoming authorities have entered a highway tunnel to begin picking through the wreckage of a fiery multivehicle crash a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The new NBA All-Star format is here, and Sunday night will show whether it works
item.description: This will be an actual All-Star matchup. On one side, there's a team with 86 combined All-Star selections, $2.7 billion in on-court earnings and where every single player on the team is either an NBA champion, an Olympic gold medalist, or both.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, KRON4

item.title: NAACP lists companies that dump DEI in its tactical spending guide for Black Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The NAACP wants Black Americans to steer their buying power toward companies that haven’t pulled back from diversity, equity and inclusion programs under conservative pressure, and the nation’s oldest civil rights organization is listing which brands have stood by — or reversed — p
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wreckage of missing plane found in Florida woods
item.description: Officials with the Flagler County Sheriff's Office are expected to remain active in the area for several days as recovery operations continue.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tours Boeing plane to highlight aircraft maker's delay in delivering a new Air Force One
item.description: President Donald Trump has toured a Boeing airplane to check out new hardware and technology features and highlight the aircraft maker's delay in delivering updated versions of the Air Force One presidential aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The 2025 Oscars won't use this 'lovely' format for the acting awards
item.description: The format was revived for the 2024 show — but it won't be repeated in the categories honoring the on-screen talent.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Army bans transgender people from enlisting
item.description: The new policies, effective immediately, could affect more than 10,000 active duty troops, per AP estimates.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: USS Harry S. Truman ship collision damage revealed in new photos
item.description: New images released Saturday show the extent of the damage the USS Harry S. Truman suffered when it collided with a merchant vessel in the Mediterranean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 20% down? The myth that could be holding home buyers back
item.description: Scraping together a down payment for a home is a challenge, especially in today’s housing market. But one persistent financial myth could be making it harder than it needs to be.
item.dc:creator: Rosie Cima, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rhode Island pantry that ran out of food gets $15K donation
item.description: A Providence pantry that ran out of food took to social media asking the community for help, and it worked.
item.dc:creator: Leah Crowley, NewsNation

item.title: Abortions to resume in Missouri after a judge blocks restrictions
item.description: COLUMBIA, Mo. (AP) — Abortions are set to resume in Missouri after a judge blocked regulations that had restricted providers even after voters approved enshrining abortion rights into the state’s constitution.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Fiery Wyoming tunnel crash kills at least 2, closes I-80 in both directions
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — A fiery pileup Friday at an Interstate 80 tunnel in southwestern Wyoming killed at least two people, seriously injured at least five others and shut down the highway.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why you shouldn't leave your windshield wipers up during every snowstorm
item.description: Propping windshield wipers up before a snowstorm is a common routine for many car owners, but according to AAA, it's not always the best practice.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Justice Department moves to dismiss Eric Adams case
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has formally asked a federal district court to drop its corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams (D) after federal prosecutors in Manhattan resigned rather than dismiss the case.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Tigers, zebras and other stuffed animals will get new homes after health concerns kept them hidden
item.description: Crocodiles, monkeys, tigers, zebras and dozens of other taxidermy animals will move to new homes after concerns about arsenic exposure forced the closure of the South Dakota museum where they had been displayed for decades.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: MISSION, Texas (AP) — An idled Border Patrol bus sat empty this week, on standby for any migrants surrendering near the southern tip of Texas. Agents in two speedboats zipped past pockets of sandy shores, known landing spots for people entering the U.S.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zelenskyy to Vance: Ukraine wants 'security guarantees' as Trump seeks to end Ukraine-Russia war
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Friday that his country wants “security guarantees” before any talks with Russia, as the Trump administration presses both countries to find a quick endgame to the three-year war.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, EMMA BURROWS and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 83 percent say president is required to follow Supreme Court rulings: Survey
item.description: Just over half, 51 percent, of Americans approve of the job the Supreme Court is doing, which is the highest amount of support it’s received in the last three years.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: Would-be Indiana school shooter had collage of mass murderers, court docs reveal
item.description: The student said in January that the shooting would be “Parkland part two” and that they had been planning it for a year.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S. to abide by a law that required TikTok’s parent company ByteDance to divest from the platform or face a Jan. 19 ban.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man accused of shooting Kansas City teen Ralph Yarl to change plea
item.description: Andrew Lester, the man accused of shooting Ralph Yarl, a Kansas City teen in 2023, will take a plea deal in a last-minute hearing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: OPM directs agencies to fire government workers still on probation
item.description: Office of Personnel Management (OPM) officials met with agency leaders across government Thursday and directed them to begin firing employees still in their probationary period a year or more after being hired.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump warns of ending Israel-Hamas ceasefire if all hostages aren't returned
item.description: President Trump on Monday suggested he might end a fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hamas if all hostages being held in Gaza are not released by the end of the week, warning that if the deal does not go through, “all hell is going to break out.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9
